title: Sanitary Pads Distribution
tagline: Keeping girls under 18 in school — distributing sanitary pads across all sub-counties in Kisumu County, completely free of charge.
intro_p1: Menstruation remains one of the leading causes of school absenteeism for girls in Kenya. Without access to sanitary products, many girls are forced to stay home during their period — missing critical class time that accumulates over months and years, putting their education and future at risk.
intro_p2: UCDP's Sanitary Pads Distribution project directly addresses this barrier. Our target group is girls under 18 years of age across Kisumu County. We procure and distribute sanitary pads across all sub-counties in Kisumu County, ensuring that no girl has to miss a single day of school because of her period.
intro_p3: Beyond distribution, our team conducts menstrual health and hygiene education sessions, helping girls understand their bodies, break stigma, and build confidence in themselves and their education.

steps.title: Identification
steps.text: We identify girls under 18 in need across all sub-counties in Kisumu County, prioritising those from the most vulnerable households.

steps.title: Procurement
steps.text: Sanitary pads are sourced and procured, ensuring a consistent, quality supply every month.

steps.title: County-Wide Distribution
steps.text: Pads are distributed across all sub-counties in Kisumu County, reaching girls at schools and community collection points.

steps.title: Education
steps.text: Alongside distribution, we run hygiene education sessions to empower girls with knowledge about menstrual health.
